evals.prompt: I just finished adding a new login page with OAuth support. Can you create a PR for this branch?
evals.expected_output: PR with feat: prefix, Summary + Testing body, PR URL returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'feat:' since this is a new feature
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
PR body '## Testing' section references screenshots or recordings (slot, placeholder, or prompt to attach) since a login flow is something visual a reviewer would understand faster from seeing
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not

evals.prompt: open a pr. i fixed the bug where the sidebar was overlapping the main content on mobile screens
evals.expected_output: PR with fix: prefix, Summary + Testing body, PR URL returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'fix:' since this is a bug fix
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
PR body '## Testing' section references before/after screenshots or recordings since a mobile layout regression is faster to verify visually than from prose
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not

evals.prompt: Submit a PR for the dependency updates I just did — bumped react from 18 to 19 and updated the lockfile
evals.expected_output: PR with chore: prefix, Summary + Testing body, PR URL returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'chore:' since this is a dependency update
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
PR body '## Testing' section references a screenshot or recording slot for test/build output, since green CI/test output is a visual signal a reviewer can absorb faster than reading prose
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool: gh CLI if available, mcp__github__create_pull_request MCP tool if not

evals.prompt: ok great, now open a PR for all that
evals.expected_output: PR composed from conversation context without running redundant git commands. feat: prefix, Summary + Testing body.
evals.context: The assistant wrote the code in this conversation: added UserProfile component, /profile route, and getProfile API call.
expectations: Skips git diff, git log, and git status since the assistant already knows what changed from conversation context
PR title starts with 'feat:' since this is a new feature
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool

evals.prompt: I rewrote the auth module to use JWTs instead of session cookies. No new features, just restructured the code. Can you make a PR?
evals.expected_output: PR with refactor: prefix, Summary + Testing body, PR URL returned.
expectations: PR title starts with 'refactor:' since this is code restructuring with no new features
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
PR body '## Summary' includes (or proposes) a fenced mermaid block illustrating the new JWT auth flow, since the request flow has materially changed and reads more clearly as a diagram
PR body '## Summary' calls out risks of an auth/sessions rewrite (e.g. session invalidation, token expiry, rollout) since the change touches auth
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool

evals.prompt: I fixed a typo in a code comment — 'recieve' to 'receive'. Open a PR.
evals.expected_output: Trivial PR with chore: or docs: prefix. Summary + Testing body, both minimal — no mermaid, no risks, no screenshots.
expectations: PR title starts with 'chore:' or 'docs:' since this is a trivial typo fix in a comment
PR title is under 70 characters
PR title uses lowercase description with no trailing period, imperative mood
PR body contains exactly two sections: '## Summary' and '## Testing' — no extra sections
PR body '## Testing' section uses checkbox format (- [ ])
PR body '## Summary' does NOT include a mermaid diagram, since there is no flow or workflow to illustrate
PR body '## Summary' does NOT include a risks/things-to-watch callout, since a comment typo touches no risky surfaces
PR body '## Testing' does NOT reference screenshots or recordings, since there is genuinely nothing visual worth showing for a comment typo
Body stays minimal overall — guards against bloat for trivial changes
Commands include 'git push -u origin' to push before creating PR
Runs 'which gh' to detect environment and uses the correct tool
